heading: Failure of selection for promotion
label: 14501 Failure of selection for promotion
notes: Amendments2006—Subsec. (a). substituted “Navy Reserve” for “Naval Reserve”.Pub. L. 109–1631996—Subsec. (a). inserted heading.Pub. L. 104–106

Effective Date of 1996 AmendmentAmendment by effective as if included in the Reserve Officer Personnel Management Act, title XVI of , as enacted on , see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 104–106Pub. L. 103–337section 1501(f)(3) of Pub. L. 104–106section 113 of this titleOct. 5, 1994
Effective DateSection effective , see , set out as a note under .Oct. 1, 1996section 1691(b)(1) of Pub. L. 103–337section 10001 of this title

Continuation on Reserve Active-Status List of Certain Reserve Colonels of Army and Air Force, , , provided that:Pub. L. 103–337, div. A, title XVI, § 1681108 Stat. 3021Oct. 5, 1994“(a).—Continuation Under Old LawExcept as provided in subsection (b), a reserve officer of the Army or the Air Force who, on the effective date of this title [, see section 1691(b)(1), (2) of , set out as an Effective Date note under ]—Oct. 1, 1996Pub. L. 103–337section 10001 of this title“(1)is subject to placement on the reserve active-status list of the Army or the Air Force; and“(2)(A)holds the reserve grade of colonel, (B) is on a list of officers recommended for promotion to the reserve grade of colonel, or (C) has been nominated by the President for appointment in the reserve grade of colonel,shall continue to be subject to mandatory transfer to the Retired Reserve or discharge from the officer’s reserve appointment under section 3851 or 8851 of title 10, United States Code, as in effect on the day before the effective date of this title.“(b).—ExemptionThis section does not apply to an officer who is—“(1)sooner transferred from an active status or discharged under some other provision of law;“(2)promoted to a higher grade, unless the officer was on a list of officers recommended for promotion to the reserve grade of colonel before the effective date of this title; or“(3)continued on the reserve active-status list under , United States Code, as added by this title.”section 14701 of title 10

Mandatory Separation for Age for Certain Reserve Officers of Navy and Marine Corps, , , provided that:Pub. L. 103–337, div. A, title XVI, § 1690108 Stat. 3025Oct. 5, 1994“(a).—Savings Provisions for Required Separation AgeA reserve officer of the Navy or the Marine Corps—“(1)who—“(A)on the effective date of this title [, see section 1691(b)(1), (2) of , set out as an Effective Date note under ] is in an active status, andOct. 1, 1996Pub. L. 103–337section 10001 of this title“(B)on the day before the effective date of this title was an officer described in section 6389(e) [now 8373(e)], [former] 6397(a), [former] 6403(a), or [former] 6403(b) of title 10, United States Code; and“(2)who, on or after the effective date of this title is subject to elimination from an active status under any provision of such title,is entitled to be treated as that officer would have been treated under [former] section 6397 or 6403 as applicable, as in effect on the day before the effective date of this title, if that treatment would result in the date for the officer’s separation from an active status being a later date than the date established under the law in effect on or after the effective date of this title.“(b).—Savings Provisions for Mandatory Separation for AgeAn officer who was initially appointed in the Naval Reserve [now Navy Reserve] or the Marine Corps Reserve before , and who cannot complete 20 years of service computed under before he becomes 62 years of age, but can complete this service by the time he becomes 64 years of age, may be retained in an active status not later than the date he becomes 64 years of age.January 1, 1953section 12732 of this title“(c)An officer who was initially appointed in the Naval Reserve [now Navy Reserve] or the Marine Corps Reserve before the effective date of this title, and who cannot complete 20 years of service computed under before he becomes 60 years of age, but can complete this service by the time he becomes 62 years of age, may be retained in an active status not later than the date he becomes 62 years of age.”section 12732 of this title

children.heading: .—Officers Below the Grade of Colonel or Navy Captain
children.label: a .—Officers Below the Grade of Colonel or Navy Captain
children.text: An officer on the reserve active-status list in a grade below the grade of colonel or, in the case of an officer in the Navy Reserve, captain who is in or above the promotion zone established for that officer’s grade and competitive category and who (1) is considered but not recommended for promotion (other than by a vacancy promotion board), or (2) declines to accept a promotion for which selected (other than by a vacancy promotion board), shall be considered to have failed of selection for promotion.

The officer is considered but not recommended for promotion a second time by a mandatory promotion board convened under section 14101(a) or a special selection board convened under .section 14502(a) of this title

The officer declines to accept a promotion for which recommended by a mandatory promotion board convened under section 14101(a) or a special selection board convened under section 14502(a) or 14502(b) of this title after previously failing of selection or after the officer’s name was removed from the report of a selection board under section 14111(b) or from a promotion list under after recommendation for promotion by an earlier selection board described in subsection (a).section 14310 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the officer is not recommended for promotion by the next mandatory promotion board convened under section 14101(a) or special selection board convened under for that officer’s grade and competitive category; orsection 14502(a) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: the officer’s name is again removed from the report of a selection board under section 14111(b) or from a promotion list under .section 14310 of this title
